title: scirpus maritimus subsp. affinis
intro: Scirpus maritimus subsp. affinis, also known as saltwort or sea club-rush, is a plant species that belongs to the sedge family (Cyperaceae) and is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The medicinal properties of Scirpus maritimus subsp. affinis include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of the plant include its ability to aid in the treatment of digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to act as a diuretic. Herbal preparations of Scirpus maritimus subsp. affinis can be found in the form of infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are typically used to treat the mentioned health issues.
